1. # **Marketing Mix Plan** {#marketing-mix-plan}

## **1.1 Product** {#1.1-product}

To build an "addictive" Gaming SaaS platform and retain users (Retention), we analyze FPT EsportHub according to the Three Levels of Product model.

### **1.1.1 Core Product**

- **Core values customers receive:** Recognition, a sense of Belonging to a high-quality community, and opportunities for development/Monetization from their passion.  
- **Problems solved:** Thoroughly resolves the pain of loneliness when gaming, frustration caused by toxic environments, and the deadlock in the development path from amateur to semi-pro/professional.  
- **Customer Benefit:** A digital "Third Place", where gamers not only entertain themselves but also build a digital career, shaping a clear and safe personal identity.

### **1.1.2 Actual Product**

| Element (Actual Product) | Product/Service Category | Detailed description of the product/service category |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Brand name** | Platform Brand | **FPT EsportHub:** Positioned as a professional and reputable esports ecosystem, fully inheriting the technological infrastructure, bandwidth, and server strength from FPT Corporation. |
| **Design** | UI/UX Design Language | **Standard eSports interface:** Uses an aggressive Dark Mode with the main color tones of Black \- Green \- Neon Purple. The User Experience (UX) is designed to be streamlined and smooth, optimizing the mouse click journey to be as short as possible, fitting the fast manipulation habits of Vietnamese youth and students. |
| **Packaging** | Distribution Format | **All-in-One Application (PC & Mobile App):** All services are centrally packaged into a single application (no fragmentation). The system covers the entire closed loop of a gamer: Play (Matchmaking) ➔ Practice ➔ Compete ➔ Stream ➔ Monetize. |
| **Features** | **1\. Smart Matchmaking System** | AI algorithm automatically analyzes match history, KDA stats, preferred roles, and communication behavior to find the most suitable teammates, completely resolving the issue of mismatched skill levels. |
|  | **2\. Communication Platform (Talk/Chat Room)** | Real-time voice/text conversation space with ultra-low latency (\<10ms), with clear administrative permissions assigned for captains and members. |
|  | **3\. Competition Space (Scrim & Premier Room)** | Provides practice rooms (Scrim) with the ability to automatically schedule matches between semi-pro teams. Simultaneously features internal ranked rooms (Premier) integrated with direct rewards (Reward-based) to create competitive motivation. |
|  | **4\. Tournament Management (Tournament System)** | Tool that automates the entire organization process: from creating brackets to collecting and updating real-time results via the game's actual API. |
|  | **5\. Broadcasting Platform (Livestream Room)** | Integrates FangTV technology with sub-3s latency. Supports Micro-streamers to broadcast matches and receive direct donations from viewers right within the ecosystem without KPI pressure. |
|  | **6\. Gamer Profile (Profile & Analytics)** | Personalized digitized eSports "CV" for gamers, storing match history, verifying actual Rank (Rank Verification), and providing in-depth analytical reports such as Heatmaps or APM. |
|  | **7\. Artificial Intelligence Filter (AI Anti-Toxic)** | AI tool analyzing Natural Language Processing (NLP) on both chat and voice logs to proactively warn against and prevent abusive behavior and game throwing, creating a safe and civilized environment. |
|  | **8\. Training Platform (Coaching & VODs)** | Archive of tutorial videos (VODs) to improve skills. Integrates a portal for hiring 1-on-1 Coaches with transparent fee tracking and commission systems. |
| **Quality level** | Operational & Security Standards | **Absolute transparency & Safety:** All achievement data is retrieved directly from the official APIs of publishers (Riot Games, Steamworks) to eliminate boosting. The system integrates hardware security and IP recognition for effective anti-cheat and anti-smurf measures. |

### 

![][image2]

### **1.1.3 Augmented Product**

- **Delivery & Credit:** Easy app download across multiple platforms. Internal e-wallet system allows Premium subscriptions (from 49k-149k VND), safe payment reconciliation (paying Coaching fees, receiving Donations, splitting prize/Livestream commissions).  
- **Installation:** Smooth Onboarding process. Features a special Student Esports Program that helps "install" the ecosystem on-site at Universities through an exclusive Campus Tour tournament series and supports the establishment of internal clubs.  
- **Warranty:**  
+ **Reputation Score:** Guarantees a "warranty" for a clean gaming environment (Low scores are banned from competing, high scores are prioritized).  
+ **Rank Verification:** Commits to authenticity, eliminating fake boosting.  
+ **AI Coaching Suggestions:** Commits to helping players improve their skills through champion/weapon recommendations after each match.  
- **After-sale service:**  
+ **Customer Support 24/7:** Exclusive privilege supporting lightning-fast resolution of tournament disputes and report complaints within 15 minutes.  
+ **Loyalty Program & Achievement System:** Retains users through digitized Badge systems and animated avatar frames for highly engaged users.

## **1.2 Pricing** {#1.2-pricing}

FPT EsportHub's pricing strategy is a "Freemium SaaS Platform", a proven strategy by Discord and FACEIT. However, we must localize it to fit Vietnam's purchasing power.

### **1.2.1 Pricing Objectives**

- **User Acquisition & Market Penetration:** Lower the entry barrier to 0 VND to gather 5,000 users in the first year.  
- **Revenue Growth:** Convert 3% \- 5% of free users to paid users in year 1\.  
- **Retention:** Use annual subscriptions/Student packages to lock users into the ecosystem.

### **1.2.2 Pricing Strategy**

- **Freemium Pricing:** Free core features (matchmaking, chat, profile creation) to build the community (Network Effect). The larger the community, the more valuable the product.  
- **Penetration Pricing:** Starting Premium price from only $2/month (around 49,000 VND) \- cheaper than a bowl of pho, perfectly fitting the budget of students.  
- **Psychological Pricing:** Use price points ending in x9,000 VND (49k, 89k, 199k) to create a sense of cheapness, stimulating impulse buying.  
- **Value-Based Pricing:** Different audiences (players, teams, tournament organizers) will receive different values, so there will be corresponding Tiers.

### **1.2.3 Selling Price Plan**

Based on the pricing of international platforms and the market reality, FPT EsportHub will implement 4 tiers:

![][image3]

*(Note: The price range of \~$2-4 USD (49k \- 99k VND) has been confirmed as the sweet spot for the Vietnamese market according to the Checkpoint 2 report.)*

## **1.3 Place** {#1.3-place}

### **1.3.1. Basis for the Distribution Strategy**

In the Marketing Mix, Place does not only refer to the location where a product is sold. It also refers to the entire system that allows customers to access, register, use, and pay for the product. For a digital platform like FPT EsportHub, the distribution strategy needs to solve two different issues.

Service distribution means how users directly access and use the platform’s features.

Access distribution means how FPT EsportHub appears in the channels and communities where the target customers are already active.

FPT EsportHub targets young gamers aged 16 to 28, especially students, semi-professional gamers, and players who want to improve their skills. This group frequently uses the Internet, social media, gaming communities, and gaming content platforms.

The project survey shows that 65.6% of respondents are aged 19 to 22, 73.1% are students, and 81.3% are based in Hanoi. In terms of gaming behavior, 74.4% play games on PC and 58.1% play on mobile devices. The survey also shows that 51.9% use Discord to find teams and communicate, while 57.5% use YouTube to watch tournaments and gaming content. In addition, 40% have never joined a tournament through a specialized platform, and 63.7% show a high willingness to use an integrated platform.

These results show that the target users are already present on digital gaming channels, but their user journey is fragmented. They use Discord for communication, Facebook to find teammates, YouTube to watch tournaments, and other platforms to compete.

Therefore, the Place strategy of FPT EsportHub should not try to immediately replace all the platforms that gamers are currently using. Instead, the project should use these existing channels as user acquisition touchpoints, then move users to the central FPT EsportHub platform to experience the integrated digital platform.

### **1.3.2. Selected Distribution Strategy**

FPT EsportHub applies the following distribution strategy:

**Direct digital distribution combined with selective distribution through gaming communities and partners.**

**Direct Digital Distribution**

FPT EsportHub provides its service directly to users through its website and application. The project does not need wholesalers, retailers, or physical stores like traditional consumer products.

Users can register, create profiles, connect game accounts, find teammates, join rooms, participate in tournaments, and make payments directly on the platform. This allows the project to directly control user data, service quality, community behavior, payment processes, user experience, Premium upgrades, and feature performance.

**Selective Distribution Through Partners**

In the early stage, FPT EsportHub does not need to appear in every gaming community. Instead, the project should focus on highly relevant partners, including university esports clubs, cyber games with active gaming communities, communities of prioritized games, small and medium tournament organizers, micro-streamers, nano-streamers, and semi-professional player groups.

Selective distribution helps the project control the quality of early users, reduce acquisition costs, and focus resources on communities that have the highest potential to create network effects.

### **1.3.3. Distribution System Structure**

The distribution system of FPT EsportHub can be divided into three layers.

**Owned Channels**

Owned channels are channels directly controlled by FPT EsportHub. These include the website, application, fanpage, TikTok channel, YouTube channel, email, and in-platform notifications.

The role of owned channels is to deliver the service, store user data, support users, and generate revenue.

**Community Channels**

Community channels are where target customers are already active. These include Discord servers, Facebook Groups, game-specific communities, student groups, esports clubs, and streamer communities.

The role of community channels is to attract early adopters, build trust, and move users toward the main platform.

**Partner Channels**

Partner channels include cyber games, universities, tournament organizers, streamers, gaming KOLs, gaming gear brands, publishers, and sponsors.

These channels help FPT EsportHub reach users in groups, organize real user experiences, and scale the platform without depending completely on paid advertising.

### **1.3.4. Website and Application \- Central Service Distribution Channel**

The website and application are the main channels where users receive the full value of FPT EsportHub.

Users can register accounts, build player profiles, connect game accounts, verify rank, KDA, and match history, find players by game, skill level, and role, join Talk/Chat Room, create or join Scrim Room, participate in Premier Room, register for tournaments, watch or conduct livestreams, follow rankings, receive rewards or donations, and upgrade to Premium.

**Reason for Selection**

First, FPT EsportHub is a digital service, so the website and application are where the product is actually consumed, not only promoted.

Second, the central platform helps solve the problem of users switching between too many separate applications.

Third, delivering the service through owned channels allows the project to control matchmaking algorithms, player data, payment systems, and community standards.

Fourth, a digital platform is not limited by geography and has a low marginal cost when the number of users increases.

**Implementation**

In the early stage, the project should follow a web-first and mobile-responsive approach instead of developing a full mobile application immediately.

This approach reduces development costs, shortens the launch timeline, allows the team to validate demand before investing heavily in an app, and lets users access the platform through a link without downloading anything. It also matches the survey result showing that 74.4% of respondents play games on PC.

A mobile application should be developed after the project proves Product-Market Fit and collects clearer data about user behavior.

### **1.3.5. Facebook Group and Fanpage \- Channel for Users With Clear Needs**

Facebook is used to reach users who are actively looking for teammates, opponents, tournaments, or gaming communities.

The main activities include recruiting beta testers, sharing trial tournaments, posting in team-finding groups, introducing Scrim Room, sharing leaderboards, announcing tournament results, directing users to registration landing pages, supporting users through Messenger, and distributing referral codes for different communities.

**Reason for Selection**

Many gamers still find teammates through Facebook Groups or personal connections. However, Facebook does not provide skill verification, behavior evaluation, or automated matchmaking.

This creates an opportunity for FPT EsportHub to use Facebook as an acquisition channel while providing a more complete solution on its own platform.

**Role in the Customer Journey**

Facebook is most suitable for the Awareness and Consideration stages.

At the Awareness stage, users see posts, videos, or tournament information.

At the Consideration stage, users read comments, view feedback, and learn how the platform works.

Facebook should not be the place where the entire product experience happens, because that would continue the fragmentation problem that FPT EsportHub aims to solve.

### **1.3.6. Discord \- Channel for Recruiting Early Adopters and Validating the Product**

The survey shows that 51.9% of users use Discord to find teams or communicate. Therefore, Discord is an important access point in the early stage.

The main activities include reaching game-specific servers, recruiting teams for beta testing, conducting user interviews, distributing early registration codes, collecting feedback after trial use, announcing test tournaments, providing technical support during the beta stage, and building a community ambassador group.

**Reason for Selection**

Discord already gathers users who have a strong need for communication and teammate finding. This group has pain points that are closely related to the solution offered by FPT EsportHub.

However, Discord should not be used as the long-term service distribution channel. The goal is to move users from manual team-finding behavior on Discord to verified profiles, matchmaking, scrim, and tournament functions on FPT EsportHub.

**Implementation Principle**

FPT EsportHub should not promote itself by spamming links in Discord servers. The project should cooperate with server administrators, organize valuable activities for the community, provide beta access, support scrim organization, and offer tournament management tools.

This approach helps the project build trust instead of being seen as an external advertising platform.

### **1.3.7. YouTube \- Content and Tournament Distribution Channel**

The survey shows that 57.5% of respondents use YouTube to watch tournaments and gaming content. Therefore, YouTube is suitable for distributing long-form content, livestreams, and proof of platform quality.

The main content includes tournament livestreams, match highlights, platform tutorials, AI Matchmaking demonstrations, Scrim Room analysis, team interviews, user stories, tournament organization tutorials, and content about fair play and anti-toxic behavior.

**Distribution Role**

YouTube does not only create awareness. It also helps users evaluate the platform before registration.

For example, a tournament livestreamed on YouTube can lead viewers to an account registration page, the next tournament registration page, team profiles, the Livestream Room on FPT EsportHub, or Premium and tournament features.

Therefore, YouTube plays the role of connecting content viewers with actual platform users.

### **1.3.8. TikTok \- Channel for Expanding Reach**

TikTok is used to quickly expand reach among young gamers, but it is not the main service distribution channel.

Suitable content includes toxic teammate situations, comparisons between manual team-finding and Smart Matchmaking, tournament highlights, skill clips, streamer reactions, 30 to 60-second tutorials, community challenges, and before-and-after content showing the difference after finding suitable teammates.

**Reason for Selection**

Most target customers belong to Gen Z, especially students. TikTok can help FPT EsportHub reach users who have not heard about the platform and encourage them to learn more.

However, TikTok mainly serves the top of the funnel. Every video should include a clear call to action leading users to a landing page, beta form, tournament registration page, or the FPT EsportHub website.

### **1.3.9. University Esports Clubs**

Since 73.1% of survey respondents are students, university esports clubs are a strategic distribution channel during the market entry stage.

The cooperation model can include providing club management accounts, supporting internal tournament organization, offering the Tournament System, giving Premium trials, building inter-university leaderboards, organizing student tournaments, recruiting student ambassadors, providing school-specific referral codes, and supporting communication for clubs.

**Reason for Selection**

One esports club can bring multiple users at the same time instead of requiring the project to acquire users one by one. This is especially important for a platform that depends strongly on network effects.

When a student community joins together, the platform can have enough users to form teams, create scrim rooms, organize tournaments, build leaderboards, and generate livestream content.

**Priority Area**

Based on the survey results, the early stage should focus on Hanoi. After the model is validated, the project can expand to Ho Chi Minh City and Da Nang.

This approach helps the project concentrate user density instead of spreading resources too widely across the whole country from the beginning.

### **1.3.10. Cyber Games \- Channel Combining Online and Offline Access**

Cyber games are selected because they gather frequent gamers who have suitable equipment and are ready to participate in competitive activities.

The implementation can include placing registration QR codes at the counter, organizing location-based tournaments, offering Premium trial codes, creating scrim rooms for specific cyber games, building customer leaderboards, combining play-hour packages with FPT EsportHub benefits, sharing tournament revenue, and organizing platform experience days.

**Value for Partners**

Cyber games can receive tournament participants, communication content, a regular customer community, opportunities to sell additional play hours and services, and a more professional brand image.

FPT EsportHub can receive high-intent users, offline tournament locations, real behavior data, community-based access channels, and stronger social proof.

### **1.3.11. Streamers and Gaming KOLs**

Streamers and KOLs are not only promotional tools. They can also become user distribution channels.

Each partner can receive a unique referral code, a dedicated landing page, a community room, a tournament or challenge, registration performance reports, and a revenue-sharing model if suitable.

**Priority Partner Type**

In the early stage, the project should prioritize micro-streamers and nano-streamers because they are more affordable than major KOLs, have more engaged communities, interact directly with followers, fit the semi-professional segment, and may also need Livestream Room and donation features.

Streamers can act both as user acquisition channels and as users who demonstrate the platform’s value.

### **1.3.12. User Distribution and Conversion Journey**

The distribution system is designed according to the following journey.

**Stage 1: Reach**

Users discover FPT EsportHub through TikTok, YouTube, Facebook Groups, Discord, streamers, esports clubs, and cyber games.

**Stage 2: Consideration**

Users access landing pages, demo videos, tournament pages, the fanpage, review content, and information from community ambassadors.

**Stage 3: Registration**

Users create an account, select games, connect game accounts, enter their role and playing schedule, and complete their player profile.

**Stage 4: Activation**

Users complete at least one core action, such as finding teammates, joining a Scrim Room, creating a team, registering for a tournament, or joining Talk/Chat Room.

**Stage 5: Revenue Conversion**

Users upgrade to Premium, join Premier Room, pay tournament fees, donate, or use advanced features.

**Stage 6: Referral**

Users invite friends and teammates through referral links, team codes, club codes, streamer codes, and reward programs.

### **1.3.13. Specific Role of Each Channel**

| Channel | Main User Group | Role in the Funnel | Conversion Action |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Website/App | All users | Service delivery and monetization | Registration, usage, payment |
| Facebook Group | Users looking for teams or tournaments | Reaching clear demand | Landing page visit |
| Discord | Gamers already in communities | Early adopter recruitment | Beta registration |
| YouTube | Tournament and long-form content viewers | Trust building | Platform or tournament registration |
| TikTok | Gen Z and young gamers | Awareness creation | Website visit |
| Esports Clubs | Students and teams | Group-based acquisition | Team creation and tournament organization |
| Cyber Games | High-frequency gamers | Real-life trial experience | Registration and competition |
| Streamers/KOLs | Followers by game community | Endorsement and viral reach | Registration through referral |

### **1.3.14. Distribution Performance Metrics**

The effectiveness of Place should not only be measured by followers or views. The metrics should be connected to the ability to bring users into the platform and create real usage behavior.

- **Channel-Level Metrics**  
+ Traffic from each channel.  
+ Cost per visit.  
+ Landing page click-through rate.  
+ Number of account registrations by source.  
+ Conversion rate by channel.  
+ User acquisition cost.  
- **Activation Metrics**  
+ Profile completion rate.  
+ Game account connection rate.  
+ First core action completion rate.  
+ Time from registration to first use.  
+ Number of users joining Scrim Room.  
+ Number of teams created.  
- **Partner Metrics**  
+ Number of esports club partnerships.  
+ Number of cyber game partnerships.  
+ Number of users from each partner.  
+ Number of tournaments organized.  
+ Retention rate after tournament participation.  
- **Suggested KPIs for the MVP Stage**  
+ 1,000 registered accounts.  
+ At least 60% profile completion rate.  
+ At least 40% of users completing one core action.  
+ 5 to 10 esports clubs in trial partnerships.  
+ 3 to 5 cyber games in trial partnerships.  
+ At least 20% of new users coming from referral.  
+ At least 30% of users returning in the following month.  
+ These KPIs are proposed targets for evaluating implementation effectiveness, not achieved results.

### **1.3.15. Risks of the Distribution Strategy and Solutions**

**Risk 1: Users may not want to leave familiar platforms**

Gamers are already familiar with Discord, Facebook, and YouTube.

Solution: FPT EsportHub should not force users to change all of their behavior immediately. The project should use existing channels to attract users while offering functions that those channels do not provide, such as skill verification, matchmaking, tournaments, and rewards.

**Risk 2: Users may be spread across too many games**

If the project supports too many games at the same time, the number of users in each game community may not be enough for effective matchmaking.

Solution: The early stage should prioritize games with strong interest in the survey, such as League of Legends and Valorant, before expanding to more titles.

**Risk 3: Network effects may not be strong enough**

If the number of users is too low, users may not find suitable teammates or opponents.

Solution: The project should acquire users in community clusters, such as clubs, cyber games, and servers, instead of acquiring individual users across the whole country.

**Risk 4: Advertising costs may become high**

Depending too much on paid media may increase user acquisition costs.

Solution: The project should increase the role of referral, partnerships, community marketing, and tournaments to create organic acquisition.

**Risk 5: User quality may be inconsistent**

Fast growth may bring toxic users, fraud, or unverified accounts.

Solution: The platform should require game account connection, use data from public game APIs, implement reporting systems, and build behavior scores from the MVP stage.

### **1.3.16. Conclusion**

The Place strategy of FPT EsportHub is built based on actual customer behavior instead of simply choosing popular social media platforms.

The website and application are the channels where the service is delivered and monetized. Facebook, Discord, YouTube, and TikTok attract users at different stages of the funnel. Esports clubs, cyber games, and streamers help the project reach users in community clusters, reduce acquisition costs, and create network effects.

In the early stage, FPT EsportHub should focus on Hanoi, prioritize students, and target communities around League of Legends, Valorant, and other highly competitive games. After reaching enough user density and validating Product-Market Fit, the project can expand to other cities and game communities.

Therefore, Place for FPT EsportHub is not simply about “putting the product on a website.” It is a multi-layered distribution system in which external channels create access, partners create community density, and owned platforms convert users into active users and paying customers.

## **1.4 Promotion** {#1.4-promotion}

### **1.4.1. Basis for the Promotion Strategy**

The promotion strategy of FPT EsportHub is developed based on the main problem faced by the target users: gamers currently have to use different platforms for different needs. They may use Discord for communication, Facebook to find teammates, FACEIT for competitive matches, and YouTube or Nimo TV for livestreaming and esports content. This fragmented experience makes the gaming journey less seamless and does not fully satisfy the needs of connection, practice, competition, livestreaming, and monetization.

Therefore, the objective of FPT EsportHub’s promotion strategy is not only to introduce a new platform, but also to communicate clearly that FPT EsportHub is an all-in-one solution for the entire gaming journey. The promotional message should focus on real user pain points such as difficulty finding suitable teammates, toxic gaming environments, lack of professional scrim systems, limited access to tournaments, and limited opportunities to earn income from gaming.

The proposed main message is:

**FPT EsportHub is an all-in-one platform that helps gamers connect, practice, compete, and create value from their passion for gaming.**

The supporting message is:

**Find the right teammates. Practice at the right level. Compete fairly. Earn from your skills.**

### **1.4.2. Advertising**

Advertising is used to create fast brand awareness and attract new users to FPT EsportHub. Since the product is still in the stage of building its initial user base, advertising should focus on young gamers aged 16 to 28, especially students, semi-professional players, and try-hard gamers.

The most suitable advertising channels include Facebook Ads, TikTok Ads, YouTube Ads, Google Search Ads, and sponsored content in gaming communities. Facebook is suitable for reaching gamers in groups and communities. TikTok is effective for generating quick awareness through short-form videos. YouTube is suitable for tournament content, highlights, livestreams, and platform tutorials. Google Search Ads can target users with clear intent, such as those searching for teammates, scrim rooms, esports tournaments, or competitive gaming platforms.

The advertising content should not only introduce product features. It should start from the actual problems of users. For example, the ads can highlight the frustration of playing with toxic teammates, wasting time searching for a team on Facebook, switching between too many platforms, or wanting to join a tournament but not having a suitable team.

Suggested advertising messages include:

**Tired of finding teammates on Facebook but ending up with mismatched players in-game?**

**One platform for chat, scrims, tournaments, and livestreaming.**

**Do not let toxic teammates ruin your gaming experience.**

**Do not just play games. Turn your skills into opportunities.**

In the early stage, advertising should prioritize highly competitive games such as League of Legends, Valorant, PUBG, CS2, and Liên Quân Mobile. Each advertising group should lead to a specific landing page. For example, there can be a landing page for finding teammates, a landing page for tournament registration, a landing page for Premium trial, and a landing page for esports clubs.

Advertising effectiveness can be measured through reach, impressions, click-through rate, cost per click, number of account registrations, cost per registration, profile completion rate, and the percentage of users who complete their first core action on the platform.

### **1.4.3. Sales Promotion**

Sales promotion is used to reduce the barrier to trial and encourage users to experience the platform. For FPT EsportHub, sales promotion should not only mean discounts. It should focus on activating core user behaviors, such as creating a player profile, finding teammates, joining a Scrim Room, registering for a tournament, or trying Premium features.

For new users, FPT EsportHub can offer a free first month of Premium, Early Adopter badges, reward points for completing player profiles, free entry to the first tournament, or free Scrim Room creation credits. These incentives give users a clear reason to try the platform immediately instead of only seeing the advertisement and ignoring it.

Referral is also an important tool because FPT EsportHub depends heavily on network effects. A gamer usually needs teammates, opponents, or a team to fully experience the product. Therefore, the referral program can be designed around team-based behavior. For example, inviting one friend to complete a profile can give the user reward points. Inviting enough members to create a full team can unlock Premium days. The team with the highest number of successful referrals can receive free tournament entry or gaming-related rewards.

For teams, FPT EsportHub can offer team-based Premium packages, reduced Premier Room participation fees for new teams, or free Scrim Room access during the first week. For esports clubs and cyber game partners, the platform can offer free access to the Tournament System for the first event or free communication support during the trial period.

Sales promotion effectiveness can be measured by the number of Premium trials, number of completed profiles, number of teams created, number of successful referrals, number of tournament participants through promotional offers, and the conversion rate from free trial to paid usage.

### **1.4.4. Direct Marketing**

Direct marketing allows FPT EsportHub to communicate with users in a personalized way based on their behavior, preferred game, rank, role, playing schedule, and platform usage status. This tool is especially important because FPT EsportHub does not only need users to register. The platform also needs users to return regularly in order to build a strong community and create network effects.

Suitable direct marketing channels include email, push notifications, in-app notifications, Messenger, Discord announcements during the beta stage, and chatbot support. For partners such as esports clubs, cyber games, and tournament organizers, the team can use email, Zalo, or direct messages to send proposals and cooperation plans.

The content of direct marketing should be personalized. For example, if a Valorant player has completed a profile but has not found a team, the system can send a notification that there are players with a similar rank and playing schedule currently looking for teammates. If a team is missing one member, the platform can remind them to complete the roster before the tournament. If a user’s Premium trial is about to expire, the platform can send a suitable upgrade offer.

Suggested notification messages include:

**There are players with a similar rank and playing schedule looking for teammates like you.**

**Your team is missing one member to join this weekend’s tournament.**

**A Scrim Room suitable for your level has just opened.**

**Your profile is incomplete. Connect your game account to increase trust when finding teammates.**

The main roles of direct marketing are activation, retention, and conversion. This means it helps users complete their profile, return to the platform, use key features, and eventually convert to Premium or paid activities.

Direct marketing effectiveness can be measured through open rate, click-through rate, profile completion rate after notification, return rate, number of room participations after notification, and Premium upgrade rate.

### **1.4.5. Personal Selling**

Personal selling is not the main tool for selling to individual gamers, but it is highly important for partners such as esports clubs, cyber games, streamers, community tournament organizers, and gaming gear brands.

The reason is that these groups can bring users in communities rather than one by one. For a platform that depends on network effects like FPT EsportHub, attracting an esports club, a cyber game, or a streamer with an existing community can be more effective than relying only on advertising to individual players.

Personal selling can be implemented through a structured process. First, the team identifies priority partners based on location, game community, and user fit. Then, the team sends a proposal, demonstrates the product, offers a trial partnership package, organizes a test tournament or platform trial activity, collects performance data, and continues negotiation for long-term cooperation.

For esports clubs, FPT EsportHub can offer a club management account, tournament creation tools, internal leaderboards, Premium packages for members, and a unique referral code. For cyber games, the platform can offer regular tournament organization, QR code registration, revenue sharing from tournaments, and promotion of the cyber game on the platform. For streamers, FPT EsportHub can provide a personal Livestream Room, referral code, donation mechanism, and support for showmatches or community tournaments.

Personal selling effectiveness can be measured by the number of partners contacted, number of product demonstrations, number of partners agreeing to trial, number of tournaments organized through partners, number of users acquired from partners, and revenue from partnership packages or commission.

### **1.4.6. Public Relations**

Public relations is used to build a credible and positive image for FPT EsportHub. This is important because gaming and esports can still face social prejudice, such as the belief that gaming is addictive, has no real value, or creates toxic online environments.

Therefore, FPT EsportHub’s PR strategy should focus on building the image of a healthy, transparent, and well-organized esports platform. Instead of only talking about features, PR should emphasize that the platform helps gamers improve their skills, find suitable teammates, compete fairly, and build a more civilized gaming community.

A suitable PR campaign is:

**Play Well, Play Fair**

This campaign emphasizes that playing well is an advantage, but playing fairly is what creates a sustainable esports community. Possible activities include a seven-day no-toxic challenge, Fair Play badges on player profiles, a positive player leaderboard, fair play tournaments, video sharing from streamers, and educational content about communication and teamwork in gaming.

In addition, FPT EsportHub can launch press announcements, articles introducing the all-in-one model, stories of semi-professional gamers, partnerships with esports clubs, community guidelines, and communication about anti-toxic and anti-cheat systems.

PR effectiveness can be measured by the number of published articles, number of brand mentions, number of partners joining the campaign, positive sentiment, number of users receiving Fair Play badges, and number of sign-ups after PR activities.

### **1.4.7. Communication Framework**

FPT EsportHub can use the AIDA model as its communication framework. AIDA includes Attention, Interest, Desire, and Action.

**Attention**

At the first stage, communication needs to attract attention by addressing familiar gamer problems, such as toxic teammates, difficulty finding suitable teams, mismatched skill levels, lack of structured scrim environments, wanting to compete but not having a team, and having to use too many platforms.

The proposed big idea is:

**You do not lack skill. You lack the right integrated digital platform to develop that skill.**

Suitable content includes gaming memes, short situational videos, TikTok clips, KOL reactions, discussion posts, and comparison videos between manual team-finding and a platform with matchmaking.

**Interest**

After gaining attention, the communication content needs to explain what makes FPT EsportHub different. The focus should be on Scrim Room, Premier Room, Talk/Chat Room, Livestream Room, Tournament System, player profiles, rank and KDA filtering, and the anti-toxic system.

Suitable content includes feature demo videos, infographics introducing the FPT EsportHub integrated digital platform, comparison posts with Discord, FACEIT, and Nimo TV, profile creation tutorials, and beta experience livestreams.

**Desire**

At the Desire stage, communication should move from “what the platform has” to “what users can gain from it.” FPT EsportHub should emphasize benefits such as finding teammates faster, practicing at the right skill level, joining tournaments more easily, reducing toxic experiences, building a profile that shows player credibility, and creating opportunities to earn from gaming skills.

Suitable content includes testimonials from beta users, stories of teams finding suitable teammates, tournament highlights, player rankings, featured player profiles, and stories of small streamers gaining their first viewers through the platform.

**Action**

At the final stage, communication needs to encourage clear and measurable actions. Calls to action should be short, direct, and connected to specific platform features.

Suggested CTAs include:

**Create your player profile now.**

- **Find suitable teammates today.**  
- **Join the beta.**  
- **Open your first Scrim Room.**  
- **Register for this weekend’s tournament.**  
- **Try Premium for free.**  
- **Invite your team to FPT EsportHub.**  
- **Become a tournament partner.**

The actions that should be measured include account registration, profile completion, game account connection, Scrim Room participation, team creation, tournament registration, friend invitation, Premium trial, Premium upgrade, and paid transactions.

## **1.5 People** {#1.5-people}

Instead of simply listing senior management (C-level) positions, this section focuses on the campaign execution team (Go-To-Market Team) to bring the 5-room ecosystem (Scrim, Premier, Chat, Live, Tournament) to customers:

Product & Tech Deployment:

- Matchmaking & Web/App Systems Engineer (Full-stack Engineer): Responsible for integrating Public Game APIs from publishers (Riot Games, Steamworks) to pull real-time player Rank/KDA data into the system. They ensure transparency and smooth operation for matchmaking features and low-latency voice chat connections.  
- Livestream & Interaction Solutions Specialist (AWS IVS Deployment Specialist): Directly deploy ultra-smooth video streams under 3 seconds, configure question synchronization features (Timed Metadata), and set up real-time donation gateways using audio or AI voice.  
- AI Anti-Cheat & Moderation Systems Engineer: Develop and monitor AI tools to detect cheating, filter toxic behavior, and protect a clean esports environment for hardcore gamers.

Growth & User Acquisition Department:

- Community Growth Specialist & Seeding Specialist: Directly searches for, builds, and attracts users from competitor Facebook Groups and Discord servers to the platform. Responsible for organizing Gaming Giveaway events. Sales Specialist: Directly approaches Cyber ​​Game owners and university eSports clubs to sell the Tournament System solution, helping them automate bracket creation and tournament management.

Customer Success & Retention Department:

- 24/7 Technical Support & Tournament Arbitrator Team (Customer Service & Tournament Arbitrator): Receives and processes complaints regarding deposits/withdrawals via Momo and VNPay e-wallets. Directly intervenes to resolve disputes regarding prize-winning match results in the Premier Room upon request.

## **1.6 Process** {#1.6-process}

**1.6.1 Overview of the Service/IT Process**  
FPT EsportHub is a digital service platform for students and young gamers. The platform focuses on esports connection, learning, coaching, scrim activities, tournament participation, livestreaming, and monetization. Therefore, the process must connect two main flows: the product development process and the user acquisition/sales process.

The process is designed to support a 12-month implementation roadmap. In the first stage, the team validates the MVP and student demand. In the next stages, the platform expands to mobile, scrim rooms, tournaments, payment, livestreaming, premium subscription, and creator/coach monetization.

**1.6.2 Product Development Flowchart**  
The product development process follows an MVP-first approach and expands by phase. Each phase adds new product capabilities while validating user demand and preparing the foundation for the next phase.

- **Overall Product Development Flow**

Market research \-\> MVP feature selection \-\> Web Beta development \-\> Closed Beta testing \-\> User feedback collection \-\> Mobile App launch \-\> Scrim and team features \-\> Tournament Management System \-\> Payment integration \-\> Livestream Room \-\> Premium Subscription \-\> Creator/Coach Dashboard \-\> Business Intelligence Dashboard.

- **Phase 1 (Month 1-3): MVP Development & Market Validation**

The first phase focuses on completing the MVP version of FPT EsportHub and validating demand within the student community. The MVP includes User Registration & Login, Basic User Profile, Community Chat Room, and an initial AI Anti-Toxic system to control negative content.

The platform also launches the Coaching & Learning Hub with a Video-on-Demand library, content categorization by game title and skill level, learning progress tracking, and course quality rating. In parallel, the Coach Marketplace Beta is developed with Coach Profiles, 1-on-1 coaching session booking, coach rating/review, and basic payment/commission reconciliation.

From the technical side, the team integrates Riot Games and Steam APIs, builds an Analytics Dashboard to monitor user behavior, and deploys infrastructure on FPT Cloud. The purpose of this phase is to build a working MVP, collect real feedback, and establish a data foundation for future community and competition features.

- **Phase 2 (Month 4-6): Soft Launch & User Acquisition**

The second phase focuses on officially launching the platform to students and young gamers while expanding the user network in Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, and Da Nang.

Product development in this phase includes launching the Mobile App for iOS and Android, implementing Scrim Room, Team Profile, Team Recruitment, Reputation System, Achievement & Ranking System, upgrading the AI Anti-Toxic system, integrating Push Notification, and optimizing the mobile user experience.

This phase helps users build regular platform usage habits through training, networking, scrim activities, and team formation.

- **Phase 3 (Month 7-9): Community Expansion & Tournament Focus**

The third phase aims to make FPT EsportHub a leading community esports tournament platform for students. The product focus shifts from basic connection to competitive engagement.

The team launches the Tournament Management System with Auto Bracket Generation, Match Scheduling, Automated Result Tracking, and Tournament Dashboard. The platform also integrates payment methods such as MoMo, VNPay, and Internet Banking. School Leaderboards, Regional Rankings, Tournament Analytics, and support tools for third-party tournament organizers are added.

This phase creates a practical esports skill development environment through tournaments and increases community engagement.

- **Phase 4 (Month 10-12): Monetization & Scale**

The fourth phase focuses on sustainable revenue and platform scale. Product development includes Livestream Room, Donation & Virtual Gifts, Premium Subscription, Creator Dashboard, Coach Revenue Dashboard, and Business Intelligence Dashboard.

The Premium Subscription includes Unlimited Scrim Room Creation, Advanced Performance Analytics, Priority Matchmaking, Premium Badge, and Exclusive Learning Content. This phase develops the Creator, Coach, and Streamer ecosystem, increases Customer Lifetime Value, and prepares the project for fundraising or Southeast Asia expansion.

**1.6.3 Sales Process/User Acquisition Process**  
The user acquisition process is built around student communities, esports clubs, university campaigns, digital marketing, creators, and referral growth. Since FPT EsportHub depends on community and network effects, the early sales process must focus on user trust, real activities, and repeated engagement.

- **Overall User Acquisition Flowchart**

Target student gamers and esports communities \-\> Launch early access Landing Page \-\> Run Closed Beta Invite-Only program \-\> Build Discord and Facebook communities \-\> Partner with university esports clubs \-\> Host workshops and campus events \-\> Launch Mobile App and referral program \-\> Organize weekly scrim challenges \-\> Run campus tour and ads \-\> Host tournaments \-\> Convert active users to premium subscribers, coaching users, livestream viewers, and creator/coach partners.

- **Phase 1 User Acquisition: Closed Beta & Community Validation**

In Month 1-3, the team implements the Closed Beta Invite-Only program, collaborates with esports clubs at FPT University and other major universities, develops a Landing Page for early access registration, and establishes official Discord and Facebook communities.

The team also organizes the workshop "How to Improve Your Esports Skills", awards "Founding Member" badges to early users, collects feedback, and conducts weekly user experience surveys. The main objective is not mass growth yet, but validation of real demand and early user behavior.

- **Phase 2 User Acquisition: Soft Launch & Campus Growth**

In Month 4-6, the team organizes the "EsportHub Campus Tour" across 20 universities, hosts Mini Tournaments and Esports Workshops, launches a Referral Program, collaborates with Micro-KOLs and Gaming Creators on TikTok, runs Facebook Ads and TikTok Ads, organizes Weekly Scrim Challenges, and implements the Student Ambassador Program.

This phase is designed to expand the user base, improve brand awareness, and build platform usage habits among student gamers.

- **Phase 3 User Acquisition: Tournament-Led Growth**

In Month 7-9, the main acquisition driver becomes tournaments. The team organizes the "National Student Esports Championship", collaborates with Cyber Game centers and Gaming Centers, allows Esports Clubs to organize tournaments free of charge, sponsors student tournaments at universities, conducts PR campaigns across Vietnamese esports communities, and expands the Student Ambassador network nationwide.

Tournament-led growth helps increase competitiveness, community engagement, and platform credibility.

- **Phase 4 User Acquisition: Monetization & Scale**

In Month 10-12, the team launches the "Upgrade to Pro" campaign, offers a 30-day Premium free trial, collaborates with Streamers and Semi-professional Players, organizes exclusive Livestream Events on the platform, partners with Gaming Gear and Esports brands, prepares a Pitch Deck for investors and strategic partners, and implements an Affiliate Program for Coaches and Creators.

This phase converts active users into paying customers and business partners while preparing the platform for long-term expansion.

**1.6.4 Sales Process Based on the Platform Model**  
FPT EsportHub does not sell a physical product. It sells access to digital services, coaching, premium features, tournament participation, livestream interaction, and partner opportunities.

- **Coaching & Learning Revenue Flow**

Users register \-\> Access VOD learning content \-\> Track learning progress \-\> Book 1-on-1 coaching sessions \-\> Coach delivers the session \-\> User rates the coach \-\> Platform reconciles payment and commission \-\> Coach gains reputation and continues selling coaching services.

- **Scrim & Premium Revenue Flow**

Users join the platform for free \-\> Build profile and team \-\> Use Scrim Room and Team Recruitment \-\> Need more advanced features \-\> Try Premium for 30 days \-\> Convert to Premium Subscription \-\> Use unlimited scrim creation, advanced analytics, priority matchmaking, premium badge, and exclusive learning content.

- **Tournament Revenue Flow**

Users/teams join tournaments \-\> Platform manages bracket, schedule, result tracking, and leaderboard \-\> Tournament activity increases engagement \-\> Sponsors and partners gain visibility \-\> Platform earns revenue through tournament packages, sponsorship, and payment-related services.

- **Livestream & Creator Revenue Flow**

Creator or streamer opens a Livestream Room \-\> Viewers watch and interact \-\> Viewers send donations or virtual gifts \-\> Platform records transactions \-\> Platform takes commission \-\> Creator Dashboard and Coach Revenue Dashboard help partners track income and performance.

**1.6.5 Process KPIs**  
The roadmap uses different KPIs for each phase.

Phase 1 KPIs: 1,000 Beta Testers, 500 VOD views per month, 50 registered Coach Profiles, 100 completed coaching sessions, D7 Retention \> 35%, and UX Satisfaction Score \> 4.2/5.

Phase 2 KPIs: 5,000 Registered Users, 2,000 Monthly Active Users, 500+ Scrim Rooms created, 100+ Teams established, Referral Rate \> 20%, and CPA \< 20,000 VND.

Phase 3 KPIs: 8,000 Registered Users, 10+ tournaments organized, 1,000 tournament participants, 50 active teams, DAU/MAU \> 25%, and 70% participant return rate.

Phase 4 KPIs: 12,000 Registered Users, 500 Premium Subscribers, 100 Active Livestream Channels, 300 Coach Bookings per month, Monthly Revenue \> 50,000,000 VND, and Premium Conversion Rate \> 5%.

**1.6.6 Budget Alignment**  
The implementation roadmap allocates budget by phase. Phase 1 uses approximately 20,000,000 VND, mainly for product development, server, community building, user testing, and events. Phase 2 uses approximately 30,000,000 VND for campus activation, digital marketing, and KOL collaboration. Phase 3 uses approximately 100,000,000 VND for tournament prize pool, event operations, PR, and media. Phase 4 uses approximately 200,000,000 VND for performance marketing, creator/streamer partnerships, premium promotion, payment, and infrastructure cost.

**1.6.7 Conclusion**  
The process of FPT EsportHub is synchronized with the 12-month implementation roadmap. The project starts with MVP validation and student community testing, then expands through mobile launch, campus growth, tournament-led engagement, and finally monetization through premium subscription, coaching, livestreaming, creators, and strategic partnerships.

## **1.7 Physical evidence** {#1.7-physical-evidence}

Digital products (SaaS) cannot be "held". Therefore, Physical Evidence is the Interface (UI/UX), Certifications, and multi-sensory experiences at touchpoints to reinforce trust for gamers and partners.

**1.7.1 Before use**

- **Landing Page & Website:** Aggressive Dark Mode interface standard for eSports (black/green/neon purple), fast page load speed.  
- **PR Articles & Media:** Articles on GameK, Kenh 14, and Facebook Communities positioning this as a "Technology product thoroughly resolving the toxic epidemic" sponsored by FPT Corporation.  
- **University Campus Events:** Practical experience booths at FPT University, Hanoi University of Science and Technology (Bách Khoa), NEU. Banners, Standees, T-shirts printed with the platform's logo.  
- **Role:** Creates an initial impression of a large-scale, official platform completely different from "flea market" Facebook groups.

![][image4]

**1.7.2 During use**

- **App Interface / Dashboard:** Integrates seamless, intuitive UX/UI (All-in-one). Modules (Chat, Scrim, Tournaments) are logically arranged on a single screen.  
- **Gamer Profile & Radar Chart:** Visually displays skill stats using beautiful spider web charts, with red and green colors indicating performance.  
- **Tournament Bracket:** Displays professional tree-style brackets similar to real World Championship tournaments, updated in real-time.  
- **Reputation Score:** Color-framed cards (Green: Good, Yellow: Warning, Red: Toxic).  
- **Livestream Interface:** Built-in overlay with a smooth donation panel, featuring fireworks effects on the screen upon receiving rewards.  
- **Impact:** Creates a "pro-player" experience for all gamers, making them feel their skills and time are valued.

![][image5]

**1.7.3 After use**

- **Achievement Badge & Ranking Certificate:** Digital certificates (in the form of NFTs or high-quality images) upon winning awards or reaching high internal Ranks, easily shareable to Facebook/TikTok.  
- **Email Reports:** Weekly emails sending "Your eSports journey this week", summarizing hours played, matches won, and number of people chatted with.  
- **Awards (Physical):** Sends physical medals or EsportHub jackets to users in the Top 10 Challenger rankings monthly.  
- **Impact:** Turns virtual achievements into real-world recognition, stimulating word-of-mouth marketing (Referrals).

![][image6]

### **1.7.4 Customer relation strategy**

- **Acquisition:** Reach out via Campus Tours, sponsoring small/medium streamers. KPI: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) \< 5,000 VND.  
- **Activation:** Ensure users find their first match in \< 3 minutes. KPI: Profile completion & first match played rate \> 50%.  
- **Retention:** Send Push Notifications such as "A tournament matching your Rank is coming up". KPI: First-month MAU (Monthly Active User) retention \> 40%.  
- **Referral:** "Invite 1 friend to play, instantly receive 1 month of Student Premium" feature. KPI: K-factor (Viral coefficient) \> 0.4.  
- **Loyalty:** Clan/Guild system. A place where members accumulate joint points for the team to exchange for items. KPI: 20% of users belong to at least 1 internal team.

2. # **Implementation roadmap (12-month implementation plan)** {#implementation-roadmap-(12-month-implementation-plan)}

## **2.1 PHASE 1 (MONTH 1–3): MVP DEVELOPMENT & MARKET VALIDATION** {#2.1-phase-1-(month-1–3):-mvp-development-&-market-validation}

### **2.1.1 Objectives**

- Complete the MVP version of FPT EsportHub focusing on connection, learning, and esports skill development.  
- Validate the demand for an esports training platform within the student community.  
- Build the initial user community and collect real-world feedback to improve the product.  
- Establish a data foundation for community and competition features in subsequent phases.

### **2.1.2 Activities**

#### **Product Development**

**Develop the Web Beta version with the following features:**

- User Registration & Login.  
- Basic User Profile.  
- Community Chat Room.  
- Initial AI Anti-Toxic system to control negative content.

**Launch the Coaching & Learning Hub:**

- Video-on-Demand (VOD) library for esports skill development.  
- Content categorization by game title and skill level.  
- Learning progress tracking system.  
- Course quality rating feature.

**Launch the Coach Marketplace (Beta):**

- Coach Profiles.  
- 1-on-1 coaching session booking.  
- Coach rating and review system.  
- Basic payment and commission reconciliation system.  
- Integrate Riot Games and Steam APIs.  
- Build an Analytics Dashboard to monitor user behavior.  
- Deploy infrastructure on FPT Cloud.

#### **Selling Plan & User Acquisition**

- Implement the "Closed Beta Invite-Only" program.  
- Collaborate with Esports Clubs at FPT University and other major universities.  
- Develop a Landing Page for early access registration.  
- Establish official Discord and Facebook communities.  
- Organize the workshop "How to Improve Your Esports Skills".  
- Award "Founding Member" badges to early users.  
- Collect feedback and conduct weekly user experience surveys.

### **KPI**

- 1,000 Beta Testers.  
- 500 VOD views per month.  
- 50 registered Coach Profiles.  
- 100 completed coaching sessions.  
- D7 Retention \> 35%.  
- UX Satisfaction Score \> 4.2/5.

### **Budget**

≈ 20,000,000 VND

- Product Development & Server: 70%  
- Community Building: 20%  
- User Testing & Events: 10%

## **2.2 PHASE 2 (MONTH 4–6): SOFT LAUNCH & USER ACQUISITION** {#2.2-phase-2-(month-4–6):-soft-launch-&-user-acquisition}

### **2.2.1 Objectives**

- Officially launch the platform to students and young gamers.  
- Expand the user network in Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, and Da Nang.  
- Strengthen player connections through training and networking activities.  
- Build regular platform usage habits.

### **2.2.2 Activities**

#### **Product Development**

- Launch the Mobile App (iOS & Android).  
- Implement the following features:  
+ Scrim Room.  
+ Team Profile.  
+ Team Recruitment.  
+ Reputation System.  
+ Achievement & Ranking System.  
- Upgrade the AI Anti-Toxic system.  
- Integrate Push Notification functionality.  
- Optimize user experience on mobile platforms.

#### **Selling Plan & User Acquisition**

- Organize the "EsportHub Campus Tour" campaign across 20 universities.  
- Host Mini Tournaments and Esports Workshops.  
- Launch a Referral Program:  
+ Invite friends and earn reward points.  
+ Redeem points for exclusive rewards.  
- Collaborate with Micro-KOLs and Gaming Creators on TikTok.  
- Run Facebook Ads and TikTok Ads campaigns.  
- Organize Weekly Scrim Challenges.  
- Implement the Student Ambassador Program.

### **KPI**

- 5,000 Registered Users.  
- 2,000 Monthly Active Users (MAU).  
- 500+ Scrim Rooms created.  
- 100+ Teams established.  
- Referral Rate \> 20%.  
- Cost per Acquisition (CPA) \< 20,000 VND.

### **Budget**

≈ 30,000,000 VND

- Campus Activation: 40%  
- Digital Marketing: 40%  
- KOL Collaboration: 20%

## **2.3 PHASE 3 (MONTH 7–9): COMMUNITY EXPANSION & TOURNAMENT FOCUS** {#2.3-phase-3-(month-7–9):-community-expansion-&-tournament-focus}

### **Objectives**

- Become the leading community esports tournament platform for students.  
- Increase competitiveness and community engagement.  
- Create a practical esports skill development environment through tournaments.

### **Activities**

#### **Product Development**

Launch the Tournament Management System:

- Auto Bracket Generation.  
- Match Scheduling.  
- Automated Result Tracking.  
- Tournament Dashboard.

Integrate payment methods:

- MoMo.  
- VNPay.  
- Internet Banking.  
- Implement School Leaderboards and Regional Rankings.  
- Develop a Tournament Analytics System.  
- Support third-party tournament organizers.

#### **Selling Plan & User Acquisition**

- Organize the "National Student Esports Championship".  
- Collaborate with Cyber Game centers and Gaming Centers.  
- Allow Esports Clubs to organize tournaments free of charge.  
- Sponsor student tournaments at universities.  
- Conduct PR campaigns across Vietnamese esports communities.  
- Expand the Student Ambassador network nationwide.

### **KPI**

- 8,000 Registered Users.  
- 10+ tournaments organized.  
- 1,000 tournament participants.  
- 50 active teams.  
- DAU/MAU \> 25%.  
- 70% participant return rate.

### **Budget**

≈ 100,000,000 VND

- Tournament Prize Pool: 50%  
- Event Operations: 30%  
- PR & Media: 20%

## **2.4 PHASE 4 (MONTH 10–12):MONETIZATION & SCALE** {#2.4-phase-4-(month-10–12):monetization-&-scale}

### **Objectives**

- Generate sustainable revenue streams for the platform.  
- Develop the Creator, Coach, and Streamer ecosystem.  
- Increase Customer Lifetime Value (CLV).  
- Prepare for fundraising or expansion into Southeast Asia.

### **Activities**

#### **Product Development**

- Launch Livestream Room.  
- Integrate Donation & Virtual Gifts.  
- Launch Premium Subscription:  
+ Unlimited Scrim Room Creation.  
+ Advanced Performance Analytics.  
+ Priority Matchmaking.  
+ Premium Badge.  
+ Exclusive Learning Content.  
- Develop a Creator Dashboard.  
- Develop a Coach Revenue Dashboard.  
- Build a Business Intelligence Dashboard for platform management.

#### **Selling Plan & User Acquisition**

- Launch the "Upgrade to Pro" campaign.  
- Offer a 30-day Premium free trial.  
- Collaborate with Streamers and Semi-professional Players.  
- Organize exclusive Livestream Events on the platform.  
- Partner with Gaming Gear and Esports brands.  
- Prepare a Pitch Deck for investors and strategic partners.  
- Implement an Affiliate Program for Coaches and Creators.

### **KPI**

- 12,000 Registered Users.  
- 500 Premium Subscribers.  
- 100 Active Livestream Channels.  
- 300 Coach Bookings per month.  
- Monthly Revenue \> 50,000,000 VND.  
- Premium Conversion Rate \> 5%.

### **Budget**

≈ 200,000,000 VND

- Performance Marketing: 35%  
- Creator & Streamer Partnership: 35%  
- Premium Promotion Campaign: 20%  
- Payment & Infrastructure Cost: 10%
